app.name: VibeCheck
app.tagline: Build healthy habits with your friends
signIn.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
signIn.emailAlreadyUsed: An account with this email already exists. Sign in.
signIn.termsNotice: By signing in you agree to the Terms and Privacy Policy.
signIn.failed: Couldn't sign in. Please try again.
ageGate.title: How old are you?
ageGate.subtitle: We need your birth year to keep things safe.
ageGate.invalidYear: Please enter a valid birth year.
blocked.title: We can't let you in yet
blocked.message: VibeCheck is available from age 13. Come back when you turn 13 — we'll be here.
parentConsent.title: We need a parent's OK
parentConsent.subtitle: You're under 16 so a parent or guardian needs to approve your account. It's the law (GDPR) and we take it seriously.
parentConsent.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
awaitingParent.title: Waiting on parent approval
awaitingParent.subtitle: We sent an email to {{email}}. Once they approve, you'll land here automatically.
focusPicker.title: What do you want to focus on?
focusPicker.subtitle: Pick 1 to 3 areas. You can change this anytime.
focusPicker.editSubtitle: Swap in what matters now. Pick 1 to 3.
tendency.title: What type of achiever are you?
tendency.intro: Two quick questions. They help us tune the app to how you actually work.
tendency.q1: When someone (parent, teacher, app) imposes a rule, you usually:
tendency.q1.a: Accept it if it makes sense
tendency.q1.b: Accept it only once I've figured out for myself why it makes sense
tendency.q1.c: It's easier when someone else is counting on me to do it
tendency.q1.d: Often push back, even when the rule is good
tendency.q2.a: To myself and others — about the same
tendency.q2.b: To the ones I've explained to myself why they matter
tendency.q2.c: To ones I've made to other people — more than to myself
tendency.q2.d: None of them — once it becomes an obligation, the appeal drops
tendency.result.upholder: Upholder — rules work on you both from outside and inside. Systems and routines suit you.
tendency.result.questioner: Questioner — you need to know why something makes sense. We'll show you 'why this works' where we can.
tendency.result.obliger: Obliger — you keep promises to others more easily than to yourself. An accountability partner will help a lot.
tendency.result.rebel: Rebel — you work best when you choose. Rankings off by default, no pressure.
profile.tendencyNotSet: Not set yet — take the short quiz.
profile.identityNotSet: Not set yet — start with who you're becoming.
profile.zenHint: For some people points, levels and leaderboards help. For others they hurt — adding anxiety or eroding the joy of just doing the thing. Quiet mode keeps habits, your identity and reflection. It removes the rest.
tendency.rebelZenBody: Rebels usually do better without rankings, points and 'prizes'. Quiet mode leaves just the meaning — you choose. You can switch it off in your profile any time.
challenge.completeBodyZen_one: You showed up for what matters for {{count}} day. That counts.
challenge.completeBodyZen_other: You showed up for what matters for {{count}} days. That counts.
identity.title: Who are you becoming?
identity.subtitle: For each focus area you picked, finish one sentence. Not a contract — change it any time.
identity.preset.sleep.cares: I'm becoming a person who protects their sleep
identity.preset.sleep.knowsWhen: I'm becoming a person who knows when to go to bed
identity.preset.sleep.protects: I'm becoming a person who guards their evenings
identity.preset.movement.dailyBody: I'm becoming a person who takes care of their body
identity.preset.movement.everyDay: I'm becoming a person who moves every day
identity.preset.movement.enjoys: I'm becoming a person who enjoys movement
identity.preset.hydration.cares: I'm becoming a person who takes care of themselves
identity.preset.hydration.listens: I'm becoming a person who listens to their body
identity.preset.mood.knowsEmotions: I'm becoming a person who knows their emotions
identity.preset.mood.selfCare: I'm becoming a person who takes care of their mind
identity.preset.mood.checksIn: I'm becoming a person who pauses to check in with themselves
identity.preset.mindfulness.stops: I'm becoming a person who knows how to pause
identity.preset.mindfulness.rituals: I'm becoming a person who has their rituals
identity.preset.mindfulness.present: I'm becoming a person who lives in the present
identity.preset.nutrition.mindful: I'm becoming a person who eats mindfully
identity.preset.nutrition.listens: I'm becoming a person who listens to their body
identity.preset.nutrition.fuels: I'm becoming a person who fuels their body well
profileSetup.title: One last thing
profileSetup.subtitle: Choose a username your friends will see.
home.emptyHabits: Add your first habit to get started.
home.focusProgressEmptyHint: Add a habit for the greyed areas to start tracking.
weeklyReview.title: This week
weeklyReview.heading: Your week in numbers
weeklyReview.noFocus: Pick focus areas to see a per-area breakdown.
habits.title: Habits
habits.freeTierLimit: Free tier allows 3 active habits. VibeCheck Pro unlocks unlimited.
habits.templatesHint: Tap one to prefill from your focus areas.
habits.targetHint: How much counts as 'done'. Leave empty for simple check-off.
habits.cueHint: Anchor the habit to a specific moment. Your brain starts to know when.
habits.cuePlaceholder: e.g. After brushing teeth, before bed, after school…
habits.copingHint: Plan B for tough days. Optional — but helps when the routine breaks.
habits.copingPlaceholder: e.g. If I'm tired, I'll do the 30-second version
habits.stackHint: Stack this habit onto one you already have. The existing habit becomes the cue.
cueTemplate.morningTeeth: After brushing teeth in the morning
cueTemplate.firstWater: After my first glass of water
cueTemplate.sittingDesk: When I sit down at my desk
copingTemplate.tiredMini: If I'm tired, I'll do the 30-second version
copingTemplate.travelSwap: If I'm traveling, I'll take a short walk instead
copingTemplate.morningBackup: If I forget in the evening, I'll do it in the morning
habits.minimumHint: A 30-second fallback. Keeps the streak, keeps the identity — just less XP. Long-press the habit tile to use it.
habits.minimumPromptBody:
{{version}}

Count it as the minimum? Streak stays.
minimumTemplate.puttOnShoes: Put on your shoes and step outside
profile.pauseHint: Illness, vacation, exam week — pause the streak without losing it.
profile.pauseActive: Active — tap to see when it ends.
pause.title: Streak pause
pause.durationHint: Up to 14 days. You can resume earlier any time.
pause.reasonHint: No need to explain — but if you want to, pick one.
pause.activeBody: Pause is active until {{date}}. Your streak is safe — nothing to do.
paused.title: Paused
paused.body: Your streak is waiting for you. Nothing to do today — take care of yourself and come back when you're ready.
envTip.sectionHint: Environment often beats willpower. Try it — if it works, you keep it. If not, drop it.
envTip.bedsideBottle: Put a water bottle on your nightstand tonight. First sip — before you check your phone.
envTip.deskGlass: A glass of water on the desk before you open your laptop. Second reflex of the day.
envTip.bagBottle: Bottle in your bag — refill every evening and put it by the door.
envTip.morningBrushGlass: Glass next to your toothbrush — drink water right after brushing in the morning.
envTip.chargerOutside: Phone charger in a room other than the bedroom. Three-night experiment.
envTip.realAlarm: A regular alarm clock, not your phone. The first 30 min of the day belong to you, not Instagram.
envTip.clothesReady: Tomorrow's clothes — ready the night before. One fewer morning decision.
envTip.bedroomTemp: Thermometer in the bedroom — keep it 18–20°C. The body falls asleep easier when cool.
envTip.shoesByDoor: Running shoes — by the door, not in the closet. Visible = possible.
envTip.gearReady: Workout clothes — laid out the night before. In the morning you just change.
envTip.backpackOutside: School backpack — in the hallway, not your room. The walk starts by picking up the bag.
envTip.stairsDefault: Stairs instead of elevator — default choice this week. Experiment.
envTip.meditationChair: One specific chair at home = your meditation chair. You only sit there during practice. Your brain starts to associate.
envTip.meditationAppFirst: Meditation app — on the first screen of your phone, instead of Instagram/TikTok.
envTip.notificationsOff: Turn off all notifications except calls — for a week, as a test.
envTip.fruitBowl: Fruit bowl in the middle of the table. Sweets — in an opaque box in the cupboard.
envTip.breakfastReady: Breakfast prepped the night before — in the fridge. 'What to eat in the morning' decided early.
envTip.sweetsHidden: Don't keep sweets out — put them in a cupboard in an opaque box.
envTip.bookOnPillow: Book — on the pillow, not the shelf. Evening contact is unavoidable.
envTip.journalBySide: Notebook and pen — by the bed. The journal starts with 'within reach'.
envTip.phoneOutOfBedroom: Phone stays in the bathroom overnight. Paper and pen by the bed for thoughts.
profile.experimentsHint: Small changes to your environment you're trying.
profile.experimentsNone: None yet — you'll see a suggestion when you create a new habit.
experiments.title: My experiments
experiments.empty: Nothing here yet. Create a habit — you'll see a matching experiment suggestion.
profile.freshStartHint: We surface a gentle card on Mondays, the 1st of each month, and your birthday. You can turn it off.
freshStart.banner.monday.title: Fresh Monday
freshStart.banner.monday.body: No big goals — one small thing you want to work on this week?
freshStart.banner.monday.bodyRebel: New week. If you feel like picking something — cool. If not — also fine.
freshStart.banner.month.title: New month
freshStart.banner.month.body: A new chapter. One small habit joining your system?
freshStart.banner.month.bodyRebel: First of the month. Only if you want to — write down what interests you this month.
freshStart.banner.birthday.title: Happy birthday 🎉
freshStart.banner.birthday.body: A new year of you. What do you want to strengthen in it?
freshStart.banner.birthday.bodyRebel: Your birthday. If you want — write down one thing for yourself. You don't have to.
freshStart.banner.semester.title: New semester
freshStart.banner.semester.body: New weeks, new day structures. Which habit do you want to lock in?
freshStart.banner.semester.bodyRebel: New semester. Only if you want — write down how you want to start it.
freshStart.ritual.monday.title: Weekly mini-plan
freshStart.ritual.month.title: Monthly mini-plan
freshStart.ritual.birthday.title: A new year of you
freshStart.ritual.semester.title: New semester
freshStart.step.identityBody: Quick look at the directions you picked. Nothing to change — just remind yourself.
freshStart.step.identityEmpty: No identity set yet. You can do that in your profile — it helps hold a direction.
freshStart.step.habitBody: Which of your habits do you want to lean into hardest this period? You can also pick 'none in particular'.
freshStart.step.habitEmpty: No active habits yet. You can add one after saving this commitment.
freshStart.step.habitNone: I'm not picking a specific one
freshStart.step.commitmentBody: Write down what you're doing this period. One sentence — no epic.
freshStart.step.commitmentPlaceholder: e.g. I go to bed before 11pm all week
profile.reflectionHint: 3 questions at end of day. 60 seconds. Tomorrow's commitment shows up on home in the morning.
reflection.title: End of day — quick reflection
reflection.subtitle: Three short questions. Each one is skippable — just showing up counts.
reflection.whatFriction: What was harder than you expected?
reflection.commitmentHint: Optional. Shows up on home in the morning.
reflection.commitmentPlaceholder: e.g. I'll be in bed by 11
brightSpot.title: Best day of the week
brightSpot.subtitle: Sunday evening. A quick look at the week — what was best and why.
brightSpot.reasonPlaceholder: What made it stand out — what you did, who with, how you felt…
challenge.convertPrompt: Want this to become a standing habit in your system?
partner.title: Accountability partner
partner.noneBody: No partner yet. Invite one person from your friends list — keeping a promise to another human hits harder than any points system.
partner.openDetails: Open to see today's progress or end the pairing.
partner.privacyNote: You only see habit names and status. Their moods, notes and reflections stay private.
partner.endBody: No drama — it's a normal part of the process. You can start again with someone else any time.
partner.requestSentBody: They'll see the invite in their Friends tab. Waiting on their reply.
badHabit.entryTitle: A habit I want to change
badHabit.entryHint: We map cue → routine → reward and suggest a substitute. No judgment.
badHabit.listBody: No judgment. Just noticing the loop makes it easier to break.
badHabit.listEmpty: Nothing here yet. If something bothers you — we'll map it without points or rankings.
badHabit.step.nameTitle: Which habit do you want to change?
badHabit.step.nameBody: You don't have to justify it. Name it how it actually looks.
badHabit.step.namePlaceholder: e.g. Scrolling my phone before bed
badHabit.step.cueBody: Pick or type what fits. More specific is better.
badHabit.cueTriggerPlaceholder: a person, a thought, a specific moment…
badHabit.step.rewardBody: No judgment — be honest. Understanding the reward is the key to changing the routine.
badHabit.reward.stimulation.title: Stimulation
badHabit.reward.escape.title: Escape
badHabit.reward.escape.hint: I can stop thinking about something hard.
badHabit.reward.comfort.title: Comfort
badHabit.reward.connection.title: Connection
badHabit.reward.control.title: Control
badHabit.reward.control.hint: I choose, no one's telling me.
badHabit.reward.other.title: Other
badHabit.rewardDescLabel: Describe it in your own words
badHabit.rewardDescPlaceholder: e.g. Scrolling makes me not feel lonely before bed
badHabit.step.substituteBody: These substitutes deliver a similar reward in a healthier way. Pick 1–3 — you'll try them and see what works.
badHabit.step.substituteEmpty: Go back to pick a reward first.
badHabit.step.substituteFootnote: Substitutes save to your list. For now as text — in a next version we'll add them as real habits.
substitute.stimulation.coldWater: Cold water on the face (30 sec)
substitute.escape.journal: Write in the journal what you're feeling
substitute.comfort.favoriteSong: Favorite song — listen all the way through
substitute.connection.checkPartner: Check in with your accountability partner
substitute.control.tidyOne: Tidy one thing in your room — your choice
substitute.control.listChoices: Write 3 things you're consciously choosing today
substitute.control.pickOneMeal: Plan one meal for today — your choice
substitute.other.nameFeeling: Name one emotion you feel right now
substitute.other.fiveSenses: Notice 5 things you see; 4 you hear; 3 you feel
badHabit.todayCheckInBody: No judgment. Just notice what happened — that alone is the practice.
badHabit.outcome.did_substitute.title: I did the substitute
badHabit.outcome.did_substitute.hint: You noticed the cue and took the new path.
badHabit.outcome.did_original.title: I did the original
badHabit.outcome.did_original.hint: OK — noticing it is a step. No judgment.
badHabit.outcome.not_triggered.title: The cue didn't show up
badHabit.outcome.not_triggered.hint: Sometimes the context shifts — that's data too.
badHabit.substituteDominant: This loop is becoming less automatic. You're doing something real.
badHabit.endBody: We keep the history — just stop asking. You can come back any time.
mood.title: How are you feeling?
mood.subtitle: No judgment. Just mark how it is.
mood.lowMoodAlertBody: Talking to someone can help. The Teen Crisis Line 116 111 is free and available 24/7.
friends.title: Your crew
friends.empty: Invite friends to see the leaderboard.
profile.title: Profile
profile.hideFromLeaderboardsHint: Nobody will see your score in friend leaderboards.
crisis.title: You can always ask for help
crisis.note: Talking to someone you trust is a sign of strength, not weakness.
progress.title: Your progress
progress.streakFreezeHint: Earn a freeze every 7 active days. It protects your streak if you miss one.
progress.noChallenges: No challenges yet. Join or create one.
achievements.title: Badges
achievements.heading: Your badges
achievements.subtitle: {{unlocked}} of {{total}} unlocked
skill.sleepMastery.name: Sleep Mastery
skill.sleepMastery.description: Consistent sleep compounds focus in adult life.
skill.physicalResilience.name: Physical Resilience
skill.physicalResilience.description: Movement builds energy and stress tolerance.
skill.emotionalIntelligence.name: Emotional Intelligence
skill.emotionalIntelligence.description: Naming emotions helps you regulate them.
skill.bodyAwareness.name: Body Awareness
skill.bodyAwareness.description: Listening to your body is a lifelong health habit.
skill.discipline.name: Discipline
skill.discipline.description: Consistency is a superpower — it transfers everywhere.
achievement.firstWeek.name: First week
achievement.streak30.name: Month marathon
achievement.sleepChampion.name: Sleep Champion
achievement.hydrationHero.name: Hydration Hero
achievement.mindMaster.name: Mind Master
achievement.comebackKid.name: Comeback Kid
achievement.comebackKid.description: You came back after a 3-day break.
achievement.firstFriend.name: First Friend
achievement.challengeFinisher.name: Challenge Finisher
achievement.level10.name: Level 10
achievement.moodJournalingWeek.name: Mood Journaler
challenge.title: Challenges
challengeTemplates.sleep7Days.title: 7-day sleep streak
challengeTemplates.sleep7Days.descriptio: Log a wind-down every night this week.
challengeTemplates.sleepBy23Challenge.de: 10 nights in 2 weeks, in bed by 11pm.
challengeTemplates.walk7Days.title: Walk every day this week
challengeTemplates.walk7Days.description: 30+ minutes of walking, 7 days in a row.
challengeTemplates.workout30Days.title: Move 12× this month
challengeTemplates.water14Days.title: 2 weeks of hydration
challengeTemplates.water14Days.descripti: Hit your water goal 14 days straight.
challengeTemplates.moodCheckinWeek.title: Mood check-in week
challengeTemplates.gratitude14Days.title: Gratitude × 14
challengeTemplates.gratitude14Days.descr: Write one gratitude note daily for 2 weeks.
challengeTemplates.breathDailyWeek.title: 7 days of breathing
challengeTemplates.phoneFreeHour14.title: Phone-free hour × 10
challengeTemplates.phoneFreeHour14.descr: 10 days of a scheduled phone-free hour.
challengeTemplates.proteinBreakfast7.des: Start every day with a protein breakfast.
challengeTemplates.allRounder7.title: All-rounder week
challengeTemplates.allRounder7.descripti: Log one habit of each category this week.
challenge.publicHint: Anyone can join. Off = only friends see it.
challenge.noPrizeMessage: Good job — others were quicker this time, but your effort counts. I'm proud of you. You'll get it next round!
challenge.noWinners: Nobody crossed the line this time, but every try counts. You'll come back stronger!
challenge.prizeStructureDuo: Both finish: 10 XP each. Only you finish: 12 XP.
challenge.prizeStructureHint: Everyone who tries has a shot — if someone does as well as you, you both get the same prize.
challenge.completeBody_one: You hit {{count}} day of the challenge. Your prize will land when the whole thing wraps.
challenge.completeBody_other: You hit {{count}} days of the challenge. Your prize will land when the whole thing wraps.
insights.aiRefreshNoFocusBody: AI tips need at least one focus area. Open Profile → Change focus goals.
insights.aiRefreshCooldownBody: A fresh AI tip is generated every 6 hours.
insights.aiRefreshCooldownBodyDev: Dev cooldown active. Try again in a moment.
tips.sleep_focus.title: Sleep is fuel for focus
tips.sleep_focus.body: Less than 7h of sleep drops focus about as much as 0.08% blood alcohol. Regular sleep is your edge in school and life.
tips.movement_mood.title: Movement shifts your mood faster than you think
tips.movement_mood.body: A 15-minute walk lowers cortisol by ~20%. When you don't feel like doing anything, try moving for a quarter of an hour.
tips.mindfulness_stress.title: Breathing is the simplest reset
tips.mindfulness_stress.body: The 4-7-8 technique (inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s) activates the parasympathetic system and slows your heart rate in 90 seconds.
tips.hydration_energy.title: Tiredness is often dehydration
tips.hydration_energy.body: Even 2% dehydration drops focus by ~10%. If you feel sleepy in the afternoon, start with a glass of water.
tips.consistency_over_intensity.title: Consistency beats intensity
tips.consistency_over_intensity.body: 10 minutes a day for 30 days > 2 hours once a week. Your brain learns through repetition.
tips.sleep_blue_light.title: Screens after 10pm delay sleep
tips.sleep_blue_light.body: Blue light from your phone blocks melatonin for up to 90 minutes. Night mode helps, but putting the phone down helps more.
tips.movement_short_walks.title: Short walks after meals steady your blood sugar
tips.movement_short_walks.body: A 5–10 min walk after eating lowers the sugar spike by ~30%. Smaller energy dips throughout the day.
tips.mindfulness_naming.title: Naming emotions makes them smaller
tips.mindfulness_naming.body: When you say 'I feel anxious' instead of 'everything's bad', your prefrontal cortex takes the wheel. It's a lifelong skill.
tips.sleep_routine.title: A steady bedtime is 90% of the win
tips.sleep_routine.body: Your body clock loves repetition. Same sleep time ±30 min for two weeks and you drift off without counting sheep.
tips.sleep_caffeine.title: Coffee after 2pm = trouble at night
tips.sleep_caffeine.body: Caffeine's half-life is ~6h. An afternoon cola or energy drink still works at 10pm and wrecks deep sleep, even if you fall asleep on time.
tips.movement_micro.title: Micro-moves count more than you think
tips.movement_micro.body: 3 minutes of stretching every hour at a desk cuts neck and back pain. Set a timer and stand up — no workout needed.
tips.mindfulness_phone_break.title: 5 minutes phone-free resets attention
tips.mindfulness_phone_break.body: Just moving your phone off the desk (not in a pocket) drops cognitive load by ~10%. A break without scrolling counts double.
tips.hydration_morning.title: A glass of water right after waking
tips.hydration_morning.body: You lose ~0.5L of water overnight through breath and skin. A glass before the first coffee clears morning fog and headaches.
tips.mood_journaling.title: Three sentences a day is enough
tips.mood_journaling.body: A tiny note — 'what went well', 'what was hard', 'what I'm looking forward to' — improves emotion regulation in two weeks. It doesn't have to be perfect.
tips.mood_sunlight.title: Daylight beats supplements
tips.mood_sunlight.body: 10 min of morning sunlight syncs your circadian rhythm and lifts serotonin. A walk to school without the hoodie is enough.
tips.mood_social.title: One real conversation changes a week
tips.mood_social.body: A short message or voice note with someone close drops stress as much as 30 min of meditation. Connection is a tool, not a luxury.
tips.nutrition_protein.title: Protein at breakfast steadies the day
tips.nutrition_protein.body: Eggs, yogurt or nuts early on blunt afternoon hunger and sugar crashes. It's not about dieting — it's about stability.
tips.nutrition_fiber.title: A colorful plate = more energy
tips.nutrition_fiber.body: Vegetables and fruits in 2–3 colors a day feed your gut, which makes serotonin. Mood starts in the gut.
tips.consistency_identity.title: Identity shifts behavior
tips.consistency_identity.body: Say 'I'm someone who sleeps 8h', not 'I'm trying to sleep more'. Your brain sticks to labels — name who you want to be.
insights.correlationTitle: What we noticed in your data
insights.correlationPositive: days with this habit — mood ran higher
insights.correlationNegative: days with this habit — mood ran lower
notifications.title: Your daily VibeCheck
notifications.body: Quick check-in time. How's it going today?
notifications.bodyFor.movement: Even 15 minutes of movement counts. Ready?
notifications.bodyFor.hydration: Glass of water and a check-in?
notifications.bodyFor.mood: How is your head today? One honest number.
notifications.bodyFor.mindfulness: Two minutes to breathe and check in?
notifications.bodyFor.nutrition: One healthy plate today — how's the vibe?
notifications.dailyReminderHint: One gentle nudge so you don't break your streak.
notifications.lowMoodAlertsHint: Gentle support when we see things are tough.
paywall.title: VibeCheck Pro
paywall.subtitle: More habits, full AI insights, private challenges. Everything that actually builds you.
paywall.safetyPromise: Safety features are always free: crisis line, hide from leaderboards, low-mood support.
paywall.termsNote: Auto-renewing subscription. Cancel any time in your app store settings.
paywall.comingSoonBody: Subscriptions go live soon. We'll let you know.
paywall.thanksBody: Welcome to Pro. All features unlocked.
paywall.privateProBody: Public challenges are free. Private ones unlock with Pro.
